Just now

--

A technical walkthrough of the tiered system of models architecture: Nemotron 3 Ultra for reasoning, Super for sub-tasks, Nano at the edge, and Foundry for identity, tools, and traces.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Orchestrating Agents with NVIDIA Nemotron Models on Microsoft FoundryThis article walks through a working architecture for long-running enterprise agents built from three parts: the NVIDIA Nemotron 3 open model family, the Hermes agent harness, and Microsoft Foundry Hosted Agents. I cover what each part does, how the pieces connect, and why the sizing decisions land where they do. You will get a component-by-component explanation of the Nemotron tiers and the architecture choices behind Nemotron 3 Ultra, a read of the reference architecture, a walkthrough of how a task moves from an email to a reviewed pull request with a skill that persists afterward, and the identity, tooling, isolation, and tracing model that sits underneath it.
Reference here.
Agent equals model plus harness
Let’s start with the definition, because most disagreements about agent architecture are really disagreements about which box you own.
